ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tweets" (
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tweet */,
  "text" TEXT /* Text content of the tweet */,
  "tweet_coord" TEXT /* Geographic coordinates of the tweet (if available) */,
  "tweet_created" TEXT /* Date and time the tweet was created */,
  "tweet_location" TEXT /* Location from where the tweet was posted */,
  "user_timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the user who posted the tweet */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the user who posted the tweet */,
  "airline_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the airline mentioned in the tweet */,
  "reply_to_tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet this tweet is replying to (if any) */,
  "is_retweet" INTEGER /* Indicates if the tweet is a retweet */,
  "like_count" INTEGER /* Number of likes the tweet has received */,
  "quote_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the tweet has been quoted */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the tweet (e.g., Twitter for Android, Twitter Web App) */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Numerical score representing the sentiment of the tweet (e.g., -1 to 1) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_airline_id FOREIGN KEY ("airline_id") REFERENCES airlines ("airline_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tweets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "airlines" (
  "airline_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each airline */,
  "airline_name" TEXT /* Name of the airline */,
  "airline_code" TEXT /* IATA code of the airline */,
  "headquarters" TEXT /* Location of the airline's headquarters */,
  "founded" TEXT /* Year the airline was founded */,
  "fleet_size" INTEGER /* Number of aircraft in the airline's fleet */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL of the airline's official website */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("airline_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Twitter handle of the user */,
  "user_timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the user */,
  "user_bio" TEXT /* Bio of the user */,
  "followers_count" INTEGER /* Number of followers the user has */,
  "following_count" INTEGER /* Number of users the user follows */,
  "verified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user is verified by Twitter */,
  "account_creation_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "profile_picture_url" TEXT /* URL of the user's profile picture */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sentiments" (
  "sentiment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sentiment analysis result */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being analyzed */,
  "airline_id" INTEGER /* ID of the airline mentioned in the tweet */,
  "sentiment" TEXT /* Sentiment category (positive, negative, neutral) */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Numerical score representing the sentiment of the tweet (e.g., -1 to 1) */,
  "sentiment_analysis_model" TEXT /* Name of the model used for sentiment analysis */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score of the sentiment analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sentiment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiments_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sentiments_airline_id FOREIGN KEY ("airline_id") REFERENCES airlines ("airline_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reasons" (
  "reason_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reason */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being analyzed */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for negative sentiment */,
  "detailed_reason" TEXT /* More detailed explanation of the reason for negative sentiment */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the reason (e.g., flight delays, customer service, baggage handling) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reason_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reasons_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "retweets" (
  "retweet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each retweet count */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet being retweeted */,
  "retweet_count" INTEGER /* Number of retweets */,
  "retweet_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who retweeted the tweet */,
  "retweet_time" TEXT /* Date and time the tweet was retweeted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("retweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_retweets_retweet_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("retweet_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_retweets_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mentions" (
  "mention_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mention */,
  "tweet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the tweet mentioning the airline */,
  "airline_id" INTEGER /* ID of the airline being mentioned */,
  "mention_context" TEXT /* Context in which the airline was mentioned (e.g., positive, negative, neutral) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mention_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mentions_tweet_id FOREIGN KEY ("tweet_id") REFERENCES tweets ("tweet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_mentions_airline_id FOREIGN KEY ("airline_id") REFERENCES airlines ("airline_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "researchers" (
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each researcher */,
  "researcher_name" TEXT /* Name of the researcher */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the researcher */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the researcher (e.g., senior, junior) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the researcher belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the researcher (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analysts" (
  "analyst_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analyst */,
  "analyst_name" TEXT /* Name of the analyst */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the analyst */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the analyst (e.g., senior, junior) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the analyst belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the analyst (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analyst_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "administrators" (
  "administrator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each administrator */,
  "administrator_name" TEXT /* Name of the administrator */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the administrator */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the administrator (e.g., senior, junior) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the administrator belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the administrator (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("administrator_id")
)
